methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $1.1B - $10.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Abridge is a Series E biotech and health company founded by Shiv Rao that has raised $1.2B+ and holds a current post-money valuation of $5.3B. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies spanning both private (7 firms) and public (3 tickers: RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) stages within the biotech and health sector.

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to Abridge's $5.3B Series E post-money, applies a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier to subsequent rounds, implying $7.4B. Peer-median triangulation across five same-sector comparables—Colossal Biosciences ($10.3B Series C), Chai Discovery ($3.8B Series B), New Limit ($3.1B Series C), Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.6B public), and Freenome ($1.1B public)—yields a median of $3.1B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $10.3B. Public-comp anchoring was not applicable due to the absence of disclosed revenue data for the target. The public biotech & health sector median trades at 6.9x price-to-sales, but this multiple cannot be applied without Abridge's revenue.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $2.6B (low), $5.3B (median), and $8.5B (high). The wide dispersion between peer median ($3.1B) and stage-progression ($7.4B) reflects meaningful uncertainty around comparable company selection and stage-transition expectations. Absent revenue visibility and deeper margin profiles, the current $5.3B valuation sits at the center of the derived range, though validation would benefit from additional revenue and profitability context.
